title: vinca major subsp. hirsuta
intro: Vinca major subsp. hirsuta, also known as hairy periwinkle or Madagascar periwinkle, is a subspecies of the Vinca major plant, a flowering perennial plant native to Madagascar and the Mascarene Islands. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of cancer, as it contains alkaloids such as vincristine and vinblastine, which have been used to develop chemotherapy drugs. In herbal medicine, Vinca major subsp. hirsuta is valued for its ability to lower blood sugar levels, improve vision, and boost the immune system, offering various health benefits. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for any medical condition.
